steps.title: Identify the context clue
steps.content: The key signal is the phrase "without betraying the vanity of the writer." Gibbon expresses concern that describing his work's success will inevitably reveal his own vanity—something private he'd rather not expose.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
"Exposing" captures the meaning here: revealing or uncovering something private. This directly fits Gibbon's concern that in describing his work's success, he cannot help but reveal his vanity.

  • A — abandonment: "Betraying" does not mean abandoning something; the passage is about revealing, not deserting. ✗
  • B — misrepresentation: The passage says Gibbon cannot avoid revealing his vanity, not distorting or falsely depicting it. ✗
  • C — exploitation: Exploitation means using something for gain; the passage describes an inadvertent disclosure, not taking advantage of vanity. ✗
  • D — exposing: Exposing means uncovering something private—exactly what Gibbon fears he will do by describing his book's reception. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
